findings.description: Open drainage hole/missing cover visible at the edge of the sidewalk/road junction in the Northeast view. The rectangular opening shows exposed depth with no cover, creating a hazard. Additionally visible in Image 1 (North) showing a similar open hole near the curb edge on the right side of the frame.

debug_points.description: The road surface shows typical urban wear with some uneven pavement and drainage channels visible, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The street appears to be a commercial area with moderate traffic and standard concrete/asphalt surfaces.

The road surface appears to be in reasonable condition with no visible potholes. The street shows typical urban wear with parked vehicles on both sides. Some minor surface deterioration is visible but no holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are present.

The road surface shows wet pavement with water pooling, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible. The dark areas in the roadway are water accumulation on intact asphalt, not holes. The road has some surface wear and discoloration but the pavement structure appears intact.

In the center-left portion of the road surface visible in Image 4 (South), there is a small pothole with visible depression and darker exposed base material. The hole appears oval-shaped with irregular edges. Additionally, Image 8 (Northwest) shows what appears to be patched repairs and minor surface deterioration along the roadway.

This location shows a construction site area with rough, unpaved surfaces near a development project (57 Obarrio/Mini Design House). The road surface visible in multiple angles shows worn asphalt with some surface deterioration and discoloration, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The large brownish areas in images 1 and 8-9 appear to be wet pavement or stitching artifacts rather than holes.

Open drainage hole/missing cover visible at the edge of the sidewalk/road junction in the Northeast view. The rectangular opening shows exposed depth with no cover, creating a hazard. Additionally visible in Image 1 (North) showing a similar open hole near the curb edge on the right side of the frame.

This is an indoor office environment on a high floor of a building in Panama City, not a road surface. The images show a modern office space with marble/tile flooring, office furniture, workstations, and panoramic windows overlooking the city skyline. There is no road or street visible in any of the images.

The road surfaces visible in all angles appear to be in good condition. The asphalt is intact with no visible holes, depressions, or missing material. There are drainage grates visible in Image 3 (East view) which are infrastructure features, not damage. The parking area and street surfaces show normal wear but no actual potholes.

The road surface appears to be in good condition. The asphalt is intact with clear lane markings visible. There are drainage grates visible near the curb in images 4 and 5, but these are intentional infrastructure elements, not damage. The green/gray blurred areas in the lower portions of several images are Street View camera stitching artifacts, not road features.

This location shows a commercial/retail area parking lot and multi-lane road in Panama City. The road surfaces visible include asphalt roadway and concrete parking areas. No potholes are visible - the road appears well-maintained with proper drainage infrastructure including metal grates (Image 3) and standard utility covers.

Urban street scene in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface shows normal wear and aging with some surface irregularities, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are visible. Metal drainage grates are present and functioning as designed infrastructure elements, not damage.

This location shows a well-maintained commercial area with smooth asphalt roadway. The road surface appears in good condition with clear lane markings, yellow-painted curbs, and designated parking spaces including accessible parking. No potholes, holes with missing asphalt, or exposed base material are visible in any of the images.

This location shows a commercial/hotel parking area with paved brick/interlocking pavement surfaces. Orange traffic cones are placed to manage parking spaces. The road surfaces visible are in generally good condition with no potholes observed - the pavement shows normal wear patterns but no holes with missing material, exposed base layer, or broken edges.

The road surface appears to be in fair condition with typical urban wear. The pavement shows some discoloration, water staining, and general weathering but no actual holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible. The darker patches visible are surface staining and normal pavement variation, not depressions.

The road surface shows general wear and some surface irregularities, but no true potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The darker areas on the pavement appear to be moisture/water staining and normal surface variation, not holes. The road has some patching and minor deterioration but maintains structural integrity.
